key_insight: SHIFT selects RLVR training data without labels or training by using one deterministic rollout to measure hidden-state shift as a proxy for instance utility.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical, low-cost way to build compact high-value RLVR datasets in domains where rewards or ground-truth answers are unavailable or expensive to compute.
method: Run a single inference-time reasoning rollout per candidate.
Compute RIRS as the start-to-end hidden-state delta to score utility.
Use quality-weighted farthest-first CoreSet selection in an RIRS-augmented feature space to preserve both quality and coverage.
evidence_signal: Across mathematical reasoning and medical QA benchmarks under ultra-low budgets, SHIFT improves in-domain accuracy and transfer to harder evaluation settings versus training-free diversity and uncertainty baselines.
watch_item: Because SHIFT depends on deterministic rollout behavior and hidden-state features, results may be sensitive to model choice, decoding setup, and feature extraction consistency.
paper_title: Single-Rollout Hidden-State Dynamics for Training-Free RLVR Data Selection
